description: Refresh the NumPy-vs-cuPyNumeric API-support manifest the pre-migration readiness assessment cross-references (read-only scrape)
rules: read-only: scrapes the upstream comparison table only; never executes or modifies the user's code
no cuPyNumeric install, no GPU, and no network to NVIDIA services required for the assessment itself — only the manifest refresh fetches a public docs URL

emits the markdown manifest to record_store; the assessment (verdict: READY / LIGHT REFACTOR / SIGNIFICANT REFACTOR / NOT RECOMMENDED) is produced by reading the user's source against this manifest and the bundled references
usage: Set SOURCE_URL (the upstream comparison-table URL; defaults to the GitHub Pages mirror). Output: api-support.md under record_store.
limitation: Does not run cuPyNumeric (pre-port check only; speedup is measured after migration). Does not auto-generate refactored code — it points to recipes. Does not profile the workload. Pattern matching can miss implicit syncs hidden in logging/decorators; read the source in borderline cases. Manifest refresh needs network access to the public docs site.
